Static read-only review found the implementation internally consistent and all boundaries intact.

Verified: (1) Live queue — next-pack-queue.json live_latest CP00-308 / live_next_unit RP09.P05.M05.S18 matches CP00-309 first_unit; pack is the legitimate next live pack. (3) Distributions — deliverable {test:1,implementation:2,fixture:6,claude_review:1} and micro-title {Permission And Audit Binding:3,Synthetic Fixture Set:7}=10 match CRM_CORE_CP309_REQUIREMENTS and the contract. (4) Contract/tests — contract current_pack=CP00-309, fixture_binding_slice_descriptor registered and deep-equal-checked against the generated descriptor; package test + validator script assert all CP309 invariants and pass per command-evidence (all exit 0). (5) Boundaries — production_ready:false / implemented_pending_review (no over-claim); Claude read-only and non-final-approver (request read_only:true, tools Read,Grep,Glob; descriptor claude_is_final_approval:false; attestation promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false); Hermes evidence/gate-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false); no-real-data/no-write enforced in code and tests. (6) CP178+ hardening — inherited forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failures, zero-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing raw-output reference, and production enterprise-trust claim; readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid JSON); no prior invalid attempts; post-review artifacts correctly pending this run. (7) Scope hygiene — unrelated dirty files (docx, ldip invalid-attempt JSON, handoff note) correctly classified as out-of-pack and not inferred in.
